mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5 percent annually while CPI growth remains above 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during periods of supply chain bottlenecks or inventory destocking.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units annually signal strong residential investment and construction sector momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of months where housing starts breach the 1.2 million unit threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal produces negative forward returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, triggering margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit potential, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, each 25 basis point increase in the federal funds rate triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat following rate hikes when inflation expectations are anchored below 2 percent.
